title: Documentation - cmd from podman_tutorial.md returns error on raspberry pi4
body:
### Issue Description

Running this command: 

```podman run --name basic_httpd -dt -p 8080:8080/tcp -e HTTPD_VAR_RUN=/run/httpd -e HTTPD_MAIN_CONF_D_PATH=/etc/httpd/conf.d \
                  -e HTTPD_MAIN_CONF_PATH=/etc/httpd/conf \
                  -e HTTPD_CONTAINER_SCRIPTS_PATH=/usr/share/container-scripts/httpd/ \
                  registry.fedoraproject.org/f29/httpd /usr/bin/run-httpd
```

 from the  [tutorial](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/docs/tutorials/podman_tutorial.md) returns this output:
 ```no image found in manifest list for architecture arm64, variant "v8", OS linux```
 When run from rabsian on a raspberry pi4.
 
 I am trying to learn podman - one hour a day - and I guess this is trivial. But I haven't been able to figure it out. I guess I could try to pull from docker hub instead of fedoraproject? Or maybe specify the platform? 


### Steps to reproduce the issue

1. Install rasbian via noobs
2. install podman via apt-get
3. try to invoke the `podman run`command above.

### Describe the results you received

Error describing that the image does not match the architecture

### Describe the results you expected

a working pod

### podman info output

```yaml
podman 3.0.1

PRETTY_NAME="Debian GNU/Linux 11 (bullseye)"
```


### Podman in a container

No

### Privileged Or Rootless

None

### Upstream Latest Release

Yes

### Additional environment details

Additional environment details

### Additional information

Additional information like issue happens only occasionally or issue happens with a particular architecture or on a particular setting
